page: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-80 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an Intel Xeon processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 80 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 40 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The system is equipped with 320.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per vCPU. Cache specifications include 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 33 MB of L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core processing capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 75092.52 and a Passmark CPU score of 52649.44. Memory performance is also strong, registering a read-only small block bandwidth of 4129.09 GB/sec and a write large block bandwidth of 94.1 GB/sec. Single-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1840.19 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1201.0. Database operations are highly efficient, as demonstrated by a Passmark database score of 22522.26 and a Redis SET throughput of 63.15 million operations per second. However, large-scale AI workloads show limitations; text generation using a 70B LLM model is weak, yielding 2.55 tokens per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the n2-standard-80 offers high resource density for workloads requiring substantial parallel processing without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. The lack of bundled local storage and public IPv4 addresses means users must provision external storage resources, which represents a key architectural tradeoff. This instance is best suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database operations, and static web serving.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-80 is a general-purpose server featuring 80 dedicated vCPUs and 320.0 GB of RAM, powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture with 40 physical cores, it delivers strong multi-core performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 75092.52 and a Passmark CPU score of 52649.44. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 4129.09 GB/sec for small block reads. However, single-core performance remains average, and the instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Tradeoffs include weak text generation speeds on large 70B LLM models. This server is optimized for multi-threaded workloads, database operations, software compilation, and high-throughput static web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-80 is an x86_64 general-purpose server with 80 vCPUs, 320 GB RAM, and strong multi-core performance, optimized for database operations and multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-80 features 80 vCPUs, 320 GB RAM, and strong multi-core performance for database and multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-density, general-purpose server featuring eighty dedicated vCPUs and balanced memory for demanding multi-threaded and database workloads.
bullet_points: 80 dedicated vCPUs and 320 GB RAM on Intel Xeon x86_64 architecture.
Strong multi-core performance with a stress-ng score of 75092.52.
High memory read bandwidth of 4129.09 GB/sec for cached blocks.
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators included.
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and static web serving.
